title: cyclopeltis novoguineensis
intro: Cyclopeltis novoguineensis is a type of plant species belonging to the family Lomariopsidaceae, commonly known as the "giant water fern" or "Venus flytrap of the tropics" due to its unique ability to trap small animals. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cyclopeltis novoguineensis include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in water or using them in traditional medicine concoctions, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
